articles.title: STILL EATING PRODUCTIVITY
articles.summary: How are global teams actually getting things done?

articles.title: THE GREAT SOFTWARE CORRECTION OF 2026
articles.summary: IGV dropped 28% from its September peak. Microsoft cratered 12% in a single day despite beating earnings. Oracle is down 56%. It's not a temporary panic - it's the market finally pricing in a structural shift where AI eats into the application layer and seat-based pricing models start to break down. Whether you agree or not, every founder selling software should understand the argument.

articles.title: WHAT FOLKS ARE REALLY VIBE CODING TODAY
articles.summary: The top use cases for vibe coding in businesses right now are rapid prototyping without waiting on engineering, building internal tools that match processes and needs, turning slide decks into real, interactive presentations, and replacing simple SaaS with custom-built solutions. Vibe coding is eliminating the gap between having an idea and seeing it work. People who need tools are now the ones building them.

articles.title: YOUR PRODUCT SHOULD PROVE ITS VALUE IN UNDER 60 SECONDS
articles.summary: Most products lose users because nothing meaningful happens early enough. Users are looking for a reason to believe. Products must earn confidence before asking for effort. Otherwise, users simply disengage.

articles.title: THE GROWTH META: EVENTS AS A GROWTH CHANNEL
articles.summary: Events should be a central part of a launch strategy. They are wildly underutilized by early-stage founders. Events are one of the highest leverage go-to-market activities. In person and live is a different type of magic - there's no way to automate a handshake.

articles.title: REACH MILLIONS OF TECH PROFESSIONALS WHERE THEY ARE PAYING ATTENTION
articles.summary: TLDR puts you in front of your target audience with almost no competition. Each newsletter features only three advertisers max, giving your message the space it deserves. Millions of developers, product managers, CEOs, and other tech decision makers actually see what you have to say! Learn more [9] about running a test campaign.

articles.summary: CRM reimagined, with context to understand and agency to act.

articles.summary: An army of analysts that never sleep.

articles.title: SPAWNED
articles.summary: Generate and launch web applications, enables discovery and engagement with a built-in audience.

articles.title: THE CASE FOR SCALING VENTURE
articles.summary: Top venture firms argue that scaling VC to raise massive funds is essential as more startups require significant capital to compete in today's market. The abundance of larger companies and increasing private valuations mean VCs must adapt, focusing on supporting founders with resources beyond just financial backing.

articles.title: RENTED VIRTUE
articles.summary: A Quaker lit a blast furnace in 1709 and accidentally started the Industrial Revolution. The businesses that endure across generations have something at their center that can't be justified in purely secular terms. The AI megacycle will produce more overcapitalized companies seeking an identity than any boom in history. The ones that last will look, to outside eyes, irrational.

articles.title: FEARLESS VS. COURAGEOUS
articles.summary: Most people assume the best founders are fearless, but the opposite turns out to be true - the great ones feel fear acutely and act anyway.

articles.title: BETTING THE COMPANY
articles.summary: Critical business moments require decisive action, often risking a company's future on one key decision.

articles.title: $340B IN VC, BUT FEWER DEALS THAN ANY YEAR THIS DECADE
articles.summary: $340B flowed into US VC-backed companies in 2025.

articles.title: THE 3 CURVES THAT MAKE A SCALABLE BUSINESS
articles.summary: You want users and revenue to grow exponentially, bugs per day to grow linearly, and support work to grow logarithmically.

articles.title: WHAT GOOD LOOKS LIKE
articles.summary: Improvement happens with less drama, fewer surprises, faster decisions, and clearer handovers when you consistently convert 'good' into artifacts and defaults.
